agenda_subtitle: Fixed operating sequence for every MD-1 report

slides.title: Executive Summary
slides.subtitle: Operating position and leadership implications
slides.management_question: What is this director's operating position this month, what is the main risk, and what is the one leadership action?
slides.action_seam: Name the single leadership action and top risk in plain language.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a full-sentence takeaway that states the operating position and next action while keeping ARR, ACV, and horizon explicit.
anti_patterns: Do not mix ARR and ACV in the same metric.
Do not bury the top action in a paragraph.
Do not show unlabeled pipeline numbers.

cards.label: All Open Pipeline
cards.body: Insert the validated all-open pipeline ARR in EUR and keep the horizon explicit.

cards.label: FY26 Active Pipeline
cards.body: Insert FY26 close-date ARR in EUR and exclude Omitted from the active headline.

cards.label: Q2 Active Pipeline
cards.body: Insert current-quarter active ARR in EUR and state if quarter coverage is thin.

cards.label: Open Renewal Book
cards.body: Insert open renewal ACV in EUR; reserve the title or subtitle for the top action and risk.

source_fields: Pipeline ARR — All Open (any close date)
Pipeline ARR — FY26 Close Dates Only (excl. Omitted)
Pipeline ARR — Q2 2026 Close Dates Only (excl. Omitted)
metric_rules: Pipeline metrics remain ARR in EUR converted.
Renewals remain ACV in EUR converted.
Top risk and top action must be grounded in validated director facts.
known_gaps: Top risk and top action are synthesized leadership readouts, not raw workbook fields.

slides.title: Q1 Promised vs Delivered
slides.subtitle: Use director-scoped actuals; qualify the baseline if promise is ambiguous
slides.management_question: What did the book deliver in Q1, what was lost, what slipped, and how safe is the promise baseline?
slides.action_seam: Explain whether the main issue is losses, slips, or baseline ambiguity and what follow-up is needed.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a takeaway about delivery versus slip while explicitly qualifying the promise baseline if it is not director-safe.
anti_patterns: Do not label workbook-global forecast references as a clean director commitment.
Do not overstate precision in the promise baseline.
Do not hide slipped deals behind a generic miss statement.

cards.label: Won in Q1
cards.body: Show won count and ARR delivered in quarter.

cards.label: Lost in Q1
cards.body: Show lost count and ARR; keep reasons factual.

cards.label: Slipped out of Q1
cards.body: Use director-scoped slipped count and ARR from validated cache history.

cards.label: Promise Baseline
cards.body: State what was promised only if the baseline is director-safe; otherwise call the ambiguity explicitly.

metric_rules: Use director-scoped won, lost, and slipped actuals.
Promise baseline must remain qualified before it is used as commitment language.
known_gaps: Workbook Q1 forecast blocks are global reference and not clean director commitments.

slides.title: Quarterly Pipeline and Forecast
slides.subtitle: Display-quarter active view, forecast mix, and omitted separation
slides.management_question: What does the operating quarter actually look like by forecast category, and if the current quarter is empty what is the explicit forward-quarter fallback?
slides.action_seam: Make explicit whether the displayed quarter is adequately supported or exposed and which forecast categories carry the pressure.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a takeaway about displayed-quarter strength or weakness, keeping Commit, Best Case, Pipeline, and Omitted semantically explicit and disclosing any fallback.
anti_patterns: Do not collapse Omitted into active pipeline.
Do not show a single unlabeled quarter number.
Do not hide a forward-quarter fallback.

cards.label: Quarter Active Pipeline
cards.body: Insert active displayed-quarter ARR in EUR.

cards.label: Commit
cards.body: Insert displayed-quarter Commit ARR from the validated outlook.

cards.label: Best Case
cards.body: Insert displayed-quarter Best Case ARR from the validated outlook.

cards.label: Omitted
cards.body: Keep omitted ARR visible and separate from the active headline.

source_fields: Pipeline ARR — Q2 2026 Close Dates Only (excl. Omitted)
metric_rules: Use ARR in EUR converted for all quarterly pipeline cards.
Keep Omitted visible and separate from active headline pipeline.
If current-quarter in-scope pipeline is zero, the slide may fall forward to next-quarter outlook only with explicit footnote disclosure.

slides.title: Quarterly Opportunity Intel and Coverage Proxy
slides.subtitle: Qualified coverage read, top Q2 opportunities, and hygiene pressure
slides.management_question: Which deals and hygiene issues most affect the quarter, and how concentrated is the execution burden?
slides.action_seam: Identify the few deals or reps that need intervention and keep coverage explicitly qualified while targets are missing.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a takeaway on concentration, risk, or thinness, but keep the word proxy or another clear qualifier until target-based coverage exists.
anti_patterns: Do not claim true coverage versus target because quota is not integrated.
Do not dump all-open opportunities when Q2 active opportunities are available.
Do not mix competitive loss themes with unrelated hygiene issues in the same sentence.

cards.label: Coverage Position
cards.body: State whether Q2 coverage is healthy, thin, or concentrated, anchored in validated ARR.

cards.label: Top Opportunities
cards.body: List the most material current opportunities with ARR, stage, and next step.

cards.label: Hygiene and Intel
cards.body: Summarize stale ARR, aging ARR, data-quality backlog, and competitor signals.

metric_rules: Coverage must be qualified because quota and targets are not available in the workbook contract.
Top opportunities should prefer current-quarter active deals where available.
known_gaps: Coverage is a proxy until quota and target inputs are integrated.

slides.title: Commercial Approval Overview
slides.subtitle: Stage 3+ control view for approvals and exceptions
slides.management_question: Is approval governance under control, and how large is the exposure still pending or missing?
slides.action_seam: State whether approval governance is controlled and quantify the exception backlog leadership must clear.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a governance takeaway that combines approval control state with the size of the missing-approval exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not bury missing approval candidates in a generic summary.
Do not mix no-approval-needed deals into the main exception story.
Do not omit ARR context for approval exposure.

cards.label: Approval Rate
cards.body: Insert validated approval rate for stage 3+ opportunities.

cards.label: Approved
cards.body: Show approved deal count and ARR.

cards.label: Pending or Missing
cards.body: Show the backlog still pending or missing approval.

cards.label: Missing Candidates
cards.body: Show the count of stage 3+ land opportunities still missing approval.

metric_rules: Approval metrics remain tied to stage 3+ land opportunities.
Use validated deal counts and ARR in EUR converted.

slides.title: Missing Commercial Approval Candidates
slides.subtitle: Land stage 3+ opportunities requiring immediate attention
slides.management_question: Which specific stage 3+ land deals require immediate action?
slides.action_seam: Use the list to assign intervention on a small set of named deals.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a message title that states the size and urgency of the approval backlog without losing the watchlist function.
anti_patterns: Do not export a raw report table into the slide.
Do not sort by owner or alphabetically; sort by largest ARR first.
Do not infer approval state beyond the validated snapshot.
body_guidance: Include opportunity, ARR, owner, stage, and next required action.
Keep the list factual; do not infer approval status beyond the validated snapshot.

metric_rules: Sort largest ARR first.
Keep the list factual and operational.

slides.title: Renewals and Retention
slides.subtitle: Current-quarter renewal book, likelihood, and watchlist
slides.management_question: What renews this quarter, what is the ACV, and where does leadership need to intervene?
slides.action_seam: Call out the specific renewal watchlist requiring leadership attention this quarter.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a renewal-risk takeaway while keeping ACV explicit and calling out sparse risk tagging when present.
anti_patterns: Do not mix renewal ACV with new-business ARR.
Do not overclaim risk precision when risk tagging is sparse.
Do not bury the named renewal watchlist.

cards.label: Open Renewal Book
cards.body: Insert renewal deal count and ACV in EUR.

cards.label: Likelihood and Risk
cards.body: Summarize renewal risk distribution and call out sparse tagging if needed.

cards.label: Watchlist
cards.body: List the renewals that need active leadership follow-up this quarter.

metric_rules: Renewal metrics remain ACV in EUR converted.
Use Q2 renewal watchlist where available.
known_gaps: Renewal risk tagging may be sparse and should be called out explicitly.

slides.title: Slipped Deals and Follow-up
slides.subtitle: Use validated slipped deals first; add root-cause depth only when owner commentary exists
slides.management_question: Which deals slipped, how large is the slip, and what follow-up is required now?
slides.action_seam: Use the slide to assign owner follow-up and distinguish known facts from still-missing commentary.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a takeaway about the size and nature of the slip; mention root cause only if the source evidence supports it.
anti_patterns: Do not promise root-cause precision if owner commentary is missing.
Do not mix slips with overdue-close backlog as if they are the same control.
Do not hide the slipped ARR headline.
body_guidance: Lead with slipped count and slipped ARR.
List the largest slipped opportunities and the stated reason if available.
If root-cause commentary is incomplete, mark the gap and route to owner follow-up.

metric_rules: Lead with validated slipped count and ARR.
Root-cause commentary must remain factual and qualified if owner commentary is missing.
known_gaps: Owner commentary is still needed for full root-cause depth.

slides.title: Salesforce Hygiene and Activity Controls
slides.subtitle: Execution hygiene, activity pressure, and concentration risk
slides.management_question: Where is CRM discipline weak enough to threaten forecast trust or execution quality?
slides.action_seam: Identify which control gaps or reps require immediate management inspection.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a control takeaway that names the dominant hygiene or activity risk and, where possible, the reps carrying it.
anti_patterns: Do not turn this into narrative filler.
Do not show counts without explaining why they matter to forecast trust.
Do not hide rep concentration behind aggregate totals only.

cards.label: No Activity
cards.body: Show the number of open opportunities with no recent activity and explain why that matters to forecast health.

cards.label: Overdue Close
cards.body: Show open opportunities past close date. Keep this operational and clearly separate from slip analysis.

cards.label: Missing Next Step
cards.body: Show the count of opportunities missing next step discipline in Salesforce.

cards.label: Total Hygiene Load
cards.body: Show total issue-points and use the slide narrative to call out the reps or books carrying the most control burden.

metric_rules: Keep hygiene and activity controls operational, not narrative filler.
Rep concentration should be tied to actual pipeline and issue burden.

slides.title: Missing Win/Loss Reason
slides.subtitle: Outcome records missing reason hygiene, with explicit rule exceptions
slides.management_question: Which outcome records still lack decision-reason hygiene, after applying the 0 - No Opportunity exception?
slides.action_seam: Use the exception list to force outcome hygiene on the highest-value missing-reason records.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a control takeaway about outcome-quality risk while keeping the explicit no-opportunity exception visible.
anti_patterns: Do not count 0 - No Opportunity rows as defects.
Do not bury the rule exception in appendix notes.
Do not show low-value rows before higher-value missing-reason records.
body_guidance: Treat `0 - No Opportunity` with no reason as acceptable.
List the most material rows still missing outcome reason hygiene.

metric_rules: Treat `0 - No Opportunity` with no reason as acceptable.
All other blank-reason outcomes remain a control issue.

slides.title: Overdue Close Date Open Opportunities
slides.subtitle: Open pipeline past close date, sorted for operational action
slides.management_question: Which open opportunities are past close date, and where is overdue-close burden concentrated?
slides.action_seam: Use the watchlist and owner summary to decide which books or reps need inspection now.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite as a control takeaway about overdue-close burden and where it is concentrated.
anti_patterns: Do not sort the owner summary alphabetically.
Do not confuse overdue-close backlog with slipped-deal root cause.
Do not show overdue rows without ranking them for action.
body_guidance: List overdue open opportunities largest ARR first.
Summarize which owners hold the largest overdue-close counts.
Sort owner summary by record count, not alphabetically.

metric_rules: Overdue open opportunities should be ranked largest ARR first.
Owner summary must sort by record count.

slides.title: Churn Risk and Finance Inputs
slides.subtitle: Hold this slide as an explicit placeholder until Finance inputs are operationalized
slides.management_question: What Finance-owned churn input exists, what is missing, and who owns the feed?
slides.action_seam: Escalate the source gap and identify the owner and next integration step.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Rewrite only as a status takeaway about source readiness; do not present fabricated churn analysis.
anti_patterns: Do not fabricate churn numbers.
Do not imply Finance coverage exists when it does not.
Do not leave the owner or next step unstated if known.
body_guidance: State what Finance input is currently available and what is missing.
Name the owner or source for the churn reporting path when known.
Do not fabricate churn trend numbers.

metric_rules: Do not fabricate churn or finance trend numbers.
known_gaps: Finance churn reporting is not yet integrated into the validated fact pack.

slides.title: Appendix and Factual Notes
slides.subtitle: Definitions, source lineage, and known limitations
slides.management_question: What definitions and caveats does leadership need to interpret the deck correctly?
slides.action_seam: Make sure ARR versus ACV, omitted treatment, and source gaps are explicit enough to prevent misreadings.
slides.title_rewrite_rule: Keep appendix titles mostly neutral; only rewrite if a limitation is material enough to headline.
anti_patterns: Do not hide material caveats in tiny text.
Do not repeat the entire deck in note form.
Do not omit source-gap disclosures such as KYC or Q1 baseline ambiguity.
body_guidance: Note that Omitted remains excluded from active headline pipeline.
Document Q1 promise-baseline ambiguity and any data-quality limitations.
State the current status of the KYC-missing Salesforce source.

metric_rules: Appendix notes should restate ARR versus ACV usage and known limitations.
known_gaps: KYC-missing Salesforce source is not yet integrated.
